rulings.Case Title: EST. OF JOSEPH A. GAGGERO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing estate will be set for 4/8/24. (A Probate Code Section 13100 affidavit must have the mandatory JC form DE-300 attached to it. Attorney costs to mail copies of a supplement to a petition are not reasonable. A supplement does not require notice and there is no statutory requirement to serve copies of a pleading that does not require notice. A petitioner does not need to serve notice to himself.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN EUGENE CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice may not be mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.1 A.] The amendment (which was, in fact, a fully amended petition, not just an amendment to the existing petition -- see CRC 7.3 for distinction) was not e-filed until 11/6/23, when it became available for viewing on the Court's website. (2) The calculation of the statutory compensation is incorrect in paragraph 31. (3) The balance shown in paragraph 36 also appears to be mathematically incorrect. (4) Did the administrator give notice of proposed action to discard or keep some of the household furniture, furnishings, and personal [e]ffects appraised at $500? [Probate Code Section 10513.] Whatever the administrator kept should be deducted from her share of the estate unless the other heir consents to the non-pro rata distribution. (5) Detailed schedules must show four columns showing the DATE of a transaction, the source or payee, the nature or purpose, and the amount... including the DATE of any sale of an asset. (6) Gains on Sale must show each separate security that was sold, not just the name of the institution. (7) The summary of account shows a different disbursements amount from the amount shown on Schedule C. (8) If the Fidelity account held just cash, how was there a gain on sale? (9) Was the administrator unable to get the Patelco funds restored that were stolen between the date of death (11/26/19) and when Letters were issued to her (7/16/20)? (How was there a Wells Fargo stolen debit from the Patelco account on 1/6/20?) (10) Who was the tax preparer? (11) What was the Morgan Stanley loan paid off on 5/23/22 at $106,579.41? (12) The attorney was paid $900 for "filing and publishing" on 12/5/19. Published notice cost $450? (13) WHO was the plumber the administrator paid $110 on an unspecified date? (14) Administrator still has cash in excess of the FDIC insured amount in one bankâ¦ (15) Account statements verifying ending balances must be for the date on or after the ending date of the accounting period, not preceding that ending date. Deadline for addressing these issues is 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. If the other heir will waive an accounting and consent to the administrator's actions and also waive notice, the administrator might want to request final distribution on waiver of accounting. (The "Supplemental" I&A filed on 10/31/23 was actually a CORRECTED Supplemental I&A that replaced the one filed on 9/7/22, and not âas requestedâ by the examiner but as required by statute. Cash is cash; there is no need to specify out of which account cash disbursements or distributions will be made. A pleading should stand on its own and not "respond" to issues raised by an examiner. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA L. STEWART REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED JULY 30, 2010
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee Kenneth Stewart And Appoint Successor Trustee And Appoint Temporary Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although the following issues (see tentative ruling for 10/09/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) Neither the petition caption nor item 1 of the DE-120 form referred to the financial and physical elder abuse claims under the Welfare & Institutions Code. See CRC 7.50 & 7.102. 3) Was a conservator appointed for the settlor? If not, does the Probate Department have concurrent jurisdiction over the elder abuse claims? See section 15657.3(a) & (b) of the Welfare & Institutions Code. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTIN SEYMOUR MORDKOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/03/2024 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/23/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 5) No proof of publication is on file. 6) The petition caption: The box "Authorization to Administer under the Independent Administration of Estates Act" must be checked. 7) Item 2d: As the petitioner is not a resident of California, a declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate, with a sufficient description of each liability and claim. A minimum bond ($20,000) is required, even if there are no anticipated liabilities or claims, and regardless of whether the will waives the petitioner's bond. 8) Item 3d: Please provide a brief description of personal property with its estimated value of $375,000. 9) Item 3f(2): A proof of subscribing witness (DE-131 form) must be filed. 10) Item 3h(2) must be checked, with the petitioner's home address specified. 11) Item 6 must be completed. 12) Item 7 must be completed. 13) Item 8: i) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents and siblings if any. ii) Please identify the decedent's sibling who is the parent of each nephew and niece listed at item 8.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IVY BEATRICE CARO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/2024. The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) The original will must be submitted. (2) Need Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E. (3) Duties of Liabilities form to include the proposed fiduciary's telephone number. (4) Petition #8 to identify the ancestor(s) of each second generation listed. (5) Proposed Order to be submitted prior to the hearing. LRSF 14.7.A. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF JOSE SANTOS CHAVEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: REPOSTED 11/16/2023 at 3:30 pm. Granted without hearing by the judicial officer after review. No appearance required.
